question_structure.context: As shown in the figure, the wooden rod is $5\mathrm{m}$ long, with its upper end fixed at a certain point. It is released from rest and allowed to fall freely (ignoring air resistance). The wooden rod passes through the cylinder $AB$, which is located $20\mathrm{m}$ directly below the suspension point. The height of cylinder $AB$ is $5\mathrm{m}$, and $g$ is taken as $10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.
question_structure.sub_question_1: The time $t_{1}$ it takes for the wooden rod to pass through the upper end $A$ of the cylinder.
question_structure.sub_question_2: The time $t_{2}$ it takes for the wooden rod to pass through the cylinder

sub_question_1.step_1: The wooden rod starts from rest and undergoes free-fall motion. The time it takes for the lower end of the rod to reach the upper end $A$ of the cylinder is: $t_{A1}=\sqrt{\frac{2h_{A1}}{g}}=\sqrt{\frac{2\times15}{10}}\mathrm{s}=\sqrt{3}\mathrm{s}$
sub_question_1.step_2:
The time for the upper end of the wooden rod to reach the upper end $A$ of the cylinder is: 
[ t_{A2} = \sqrt{\frac{2h_{A2}}{g}} = \sqrt{\frac{2 \times 20}{10}} \, \mathrm{s} = 2 \, \mathrm{s} ]
sub_question_1.step_3: The time it takes for the wooden rod to pass through the upper end $A$ of the cylinder: $t_{1}=t_{A2}-t_{A1}=(2-\sqrt{3})\mathrm{s}$

sub_question_2.step_4: The time it takes for the lower end of the wooden rod to reach the upper end $A$ of the cylinder is: $t_{A1}=\sqrt{3}\mathrm{s}$
sub_question_2.step_5: The time it takes for the upper end of the wooden rod to leave the lower end $B$ of the cylinder is: $t_{B}=\sqrt{\frac{2h_{B}}{g}}=\sqrt{\frac{2\times25}{10}}\mathrm{s}=\sqrt{5}\mathrm{s}$
sub_question_2.step_6: The time it takes for the wooden rod to pass through the cylinder: $t_{2}=t_{B}-t_{A1}=(\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3})\mathrm{s}$

image_captions: The image depicts a vertical line drawing with a rectangular object hanging from a horizontal line at the top. Below, a distance of 20 meters, is a cylindrical object labeled A at the top and B at the bottom.

step_1.physical_theorem: Free fall motion with initial velocity of 0

result_quantity.name: Time for lower end of rod to reach A

Time for rod to pass through A

step_4.physical_theorem: Repeating the result from the previous calculation

result_quantity.name: Time for lower end of rod to reach A

step_5.physical_theorem: Free fall motion with initial velocity of 0

result_quantity.name: Time for upper end of rod to reach B
Time for rod to pass through cylinder
